verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted growth factor that exists in multiple isoforms. The predominant circulating form is soluble and acts on cognate receptors (VEGFR1/2, NRP1/2) on endothelial and other cells. However, VEGFA has documented cell-surface-anchored isoforms (particularly VEGFA121 and longer variants that remain associated with the extracellular matrix and cell surface via heparan sulfate binding and NRP co-receptors in a non-covalent but wash-resistant manner). More critically, anti-VEGFA biologics (bevacizumab, aflibercept, etc.) are deployed as cancer therapeutics precisely because they engage VEGFA in the extracellular space during active angiogenesis. While the dominant therapeutic target is the circulating secreted pool, VEGFA also deposits stably in the ECM and tethers to cell-surface heparan sulfate proteoglycans in a manner that permits antibody access. This is a dual_localization scenario: predominant secreted cytokine, but with documented stable extracellular matrix and pericellular surface deposition, making it accessible to binders in situ during tissue remodeling.
